title: Piquant Pan Filipino Fish Onigiri
description: A spicy and aerated Filipino dish featuring strong white bread flour and nigiri-shaping, perfect for a pescatarian-friendly brunch
instructions: Combine strong white bread flour, yeast, salt, sugar, and fish paste in a large mixing bowl.
Gradually add water to the mixture and knead for 10 minutes until a smooth dough forms.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 8 equal pieces.
Shape each piece into a triangle and place a slice of chili pepper and a sprinkle of green onions in the center.
Fold the triangle to form a nigiri shape and press the edges together to seal.
Heat sesame oil in a non-stick pan over medium heat and cook the onigiri for 3-4 minutes on each side.
Brush the onigiri with calamansi juice and serve immediately.
